mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 6.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days regardless of current rate levels.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in greater than 50% of high-inflation months, or the 5-day return correlation with CPIAUCSL spikes is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200,000 confirm robust economic momentum and earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by > 200k in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in greater than 55% of months with greater than 200k payroll gains, or the signal fails to predict returns during periods of labor market saturation.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant real GDP growth combined with rising inflation creates stagflationary conditions that compress profit margins and equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive forward returns in greater than 60% of cases, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its 2017 baseline and unemployment falls below 6%, tight labor and supply conditions signal peak-cycle earnings strength, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 50% of peak-cycle observations, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when FEDFUNDS rises above 2.5%.
